question: 1. Display all department numbers from both EMP and DEPT tables using UNION.
query: SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP UNION SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.DEPT;

question: 2. Display all department numbers FROM EMP and DEPT including duplicates using UNION ALL.
query: SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP UNION ALL SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.DEPT;

question: 3. Display department numbers that are present in both EMP and DEPT using INTERSECT.
query: SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP INTERSECT SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.DEPT;

question: 4. Display department numbers that are present in EMP but not in DEPT using MINUS.
query: SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP MINUS SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.DEPT;

question: 5. Display department numbers that are present in DEPT but not assigned to any employee.
query: SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.DEPT MINUS SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP;

question: 6. Display employee names and department names in a single column using UNION.
query: SELECT ENAME AS NAME FROM SCOTT.EMP UNION SELECT DNAME AS NAME FROM SCOTT.DEPT;

question: 7. Display all employee names and department names, including duplicates.
query: SELECT ENAME AS NAME FROM SCOTT.EMP UNION ALL SELECT DNAME AS NAME FROM SCOTT.DEPT;

question: 8. Display jobs that exist in EMP table and also exist as department names in DEPT table (use INTERSECT).
query: SELECT JOB FROM SCOTT.EMP INTERSECT SELECT DNAME FROM SCOTT.DEPT;

question: 9. Display employee names that are not department names.
query: SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP MINUS SELECT DNAME FROM SCOTT.DEPT;

question: 10. Display department locations that are not used by any employee.
query: SELECT LOC FROM SCOTT.DEPT MINUS SELECT D.LOC FROM SCOTT.DEPT D JOIN SCOTT.EMP E ON D.DEPTNO = E.DEPTNO;

question: 11. Display employee names from department 10 and department 20 using UNION.
query: SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO = 10 UNION SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO = 20;

question: 12. Display employee names from department 10 and department 20 including duplicates.
query: SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO = 10 UNION ALL SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO = 20;

question: 13. Display employees who work in departments that exist in DEPT table using INTERSECT.
query: SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO IN (SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP INTERSECT SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.DEPT);

question: 14. Display employees whose department does not exist in DEPT table using MINUS.
query: SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO IN (SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP MINUS SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.DEPT);

question: 15. Display department numbers that have employees and also exist in DEPT table.
query: SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.DEPT INTERSECT SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP;

question: 16. Display all unique job roles FROM EMP and all unique department names from DEPT in one result.
query: SELECT JOB AS ROLE FROM SCOTT.EMP UNION SELECT DNAME AS ROLE FROM SCOTT.DEPT;

question: 17. Display common values between employee job and department location.
query: SELECT JOB FROM SCOTT.EMP INTERSECT SELECT LOC FROM SCOTT.DEPT;

question: 18. Display employee names who are not assigned to any valid department.
query: SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP MINUS SELECT E.ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP E JOIN SCOTT.DEPT D ON E.DEPTNO = D.DEPTNO;

question: 19. Combine employees earning more than 2000 and employees from department 10 using UNION.
query: SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL > 2000 UNION SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO = 10;

question: 20. Display employees who work in department 10 but not in department 20 using MINUS.
query: SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO = 10 MINUS SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO = 20;

question: 21. Display department numbers that exist in both EMP and DEPT, but exclude department 10.
query: (SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP INTERSECT SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.DEPT) MINUS SELECT 10 AS DEPTNO FROM DUAL;

question: 22. Display all employee names and department names in a single result sorted alphabetically.
query: SELECT ENAME AS NAME FROM SCOTT.EMP UNION SELECT DNAME AS NAME FROM SCOTT.DEPT ORDER BY 1;

question: 23. Display department numbers that appear in EMP more than once using UNION ALL.
query: SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.EMP GROUP BY DEPTNO HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 UNION ALL SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.DEPT WHERE 1 = 0;

question: 24. Display employees from departments located in NEW YORK and DALLAS using UNION.
query: SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO IN (SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.DEPT WHERE LOC = 'NEW YORK') UNION SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE DEPTNO IN (SELECT DEPTNO FROM SCOTT.DEPT WHERE LOC = 'DALLAS');

question: 25. Combine results of employees with salary > 3000 and employees with job = 'MANAGER', removing duplicates.
query: SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE SAL > 3000 UNION SELECT ENAME FROM SCOTT.EMP WHERE JOB = 'MANAGER';
